paper_identity.title: Filtered − DiskANN: Graph Algorithms for Approximate Nearest Neighbor Search with Filters
paper_identity.venue: Proceedings of the ACM Web Conference 2023 (WWW '23)

scientific_problem.problem: Inefficient execution of vector similarity searches subject to hard metadata filter constraints.
scientific_problem.motivation: Web scale search and advertising need low query latency combined with high target recall precision values.
scientific_problem.gap: Prior index systems optimize search runtime filtering steps but do not adapt the graph's structural edge layout during construction.
scientific_problem.hypothesis: Embedding metadata properties directly into build-time edge selection functions preserves long-range routing connectivity for highly selective properties.
scientific_problem.research_question: Can metadata-aware graph topologies maintain high QPS configurations across extreme filter selectivities without inflating index sizes?
scientific_problem.success_criteria: Outperforming standard IVF inline methods, HNSW setups, and alternative graph systems under strict response latency conditions.

concepts.name: Filter Specificity
concepts.role: Quantifies the ratio of elements matching a metadata property filter relative to the full dataset size.

concepts.name: FilteredRobustPrune
concepts.role: Pruning criteria that drops redundant spatial edges while preserving metadata-navigable routing paths.

methods.name: FilteredVamana
methods.role: Incremental graph compiler that applies metadata pruning filters iteratively as individual elements are indexed.
methods.input: Dataset vector arrays, alpha index distribution settings, search scale arrays.
methods.output: Integrated unified directed search graph topology configuration.
methods.claimed_advantage: Faster indexing execution times and native support for continuous incremental vector data additions.
methods.limitation: Exhibits lower absolute query throughput capacity relative to the StitchedVamana variant.

methods.name: StitchedVamana
methods.role: Batch graph index builder that cross-links independent categorical sub-graphs and applies global pruning constraints.
methods.input: Categorical partition arrays, small parameter limit definitions, target structural threshold caps.
methods.output: Cross-stitched structural lookup graph index architecture layout.
methods.claimed_advantage: Maximizes search throughput across highly competitive accuracy boundaries.
methods.limitation: Requires high indexing build times and lacks a native mechanism for real-time item deletions.

metrics.name: Recall@10
metrics.definition: The proportion of the true top-10 filtered nearest neighbors captured in the retrieved candidate set.

metrics.name: Queries Per Second (QPS)
metrics.definition: The number of search operations processed per second under multi-threaded execution.
metrics.reported_value: Varies across charts (up to 10^5 factor improvements)

baselines.name: IVF Inline-Processing
baselines.fairness: Highly competitive; tuned across multiple cluster configurations up to 8192 centroids.

baselines.name: NHQ KGraph
baselines.fairness: Evaluated using public research implementations under identical system runtime parameters.

claims.claim: Metadata-driven graph index topologies provide an order-of-magnitude QPS improvement over search-time filtering alternatives.
claims.evidence: Verified across Turing, Prep, and DANN evaluation benchmarks under varying filter selectivities.
claims.risk: Performance gains depend on target attributes matching index distributions.

limitations.limitation: Fails to support logical conjunction combinations (AND operations) natively inside graph optimization pruning loops.
limitations.evidence: Scope defined strictly to single exact matches; compound logical terms require downstream post-merges.

research_gaps.gap: Lack of dynamic index repair strategies for point deletions in metadata-aware proximity graphs.
research_gaps.opportunity: Formulating structural repair criteria that preserve graph connectivity without requiring local sub-graph rebuilds.
